title: coronopus niloticus subsp. lepidioides
intro: Coronopus niloticus subsp. lepidioides, also known as Nile valerian or Lepidioid watercress, is a type of flowering plant in the Brassicaceae family. It is traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, due to its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The plant is also valued for its potential health benefits, including lowering blood pressure and improving liver function. Coronopus niloticus subsp. lepidioides can be consumed as an herbal preparation, often in the form of tea, infusion, or tincture, although its effects and safety should be verified by a medical professional before use.
